findings.problem: This is a comedy-driven humiliation chapter, but the narration repeatedly explains why the crowd's corrections and jokes land instead of trusting action, call-and-response, and fallout to do that work. Those interpretive glosses slightly deflate the comic pressure and slow the humiliation engine at moments that should stay sharper and meaner.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the explanatory beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:175, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:337 so the social logic arrives through observable behavior: who repeats the name, what surface changes, who laughs, who goes quiet, and who seizes the story. Keep the crowd-specific causality, but remove or recast enough narrator explanation that the scene lands as farce in motion rather than farce plus commentary.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:175, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:337 let a reader infer why 'Wakeblue' and 'Bridelight' spread and why Damaris has the story from on-page reactions alone, with no more than one explicit narrator-explanation sentence per passage; FAIL if the narration still repeatedly tells the reader why the joke or correction is working.

findings.problem: The chapter leans on the same corrective sentence shape at key turns: a negation or downgrade followed by the 'real' version of the beat. Used this often, that reversal stops feeling precise and starts reading like a default emphasis pattern, which flattens some otherwise strong prose.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the turn sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:155, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:337, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:357, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:417 so at least three of those beats land through fresher syntax. Let one turn land by image, one by blunt declarative force, and one by action or sensory residue instead of another negative-corrective reversal.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, across the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:155, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:337, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:357, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:417, no more than two emotional pivots still use the current negation-to-correction pattern and each revised sentence reads as syntactically distinct on the page; FAIL if three or more of those moments still rely on the same reversal shape.

summary: Booker and dialogue pass. The launch lands as self-important commercial spectacle collapsing into socially legible farce, the chapter stays in canonical close-third past tense, voices remain distinct and aligned with the style bible, and I found no continuity contradiction, tense drift, sanitization, timidity, intimacy fade-to-black, or example-line copying. Craft and prose fail on two unresolved medium issues: the chapter over-explains several comic beats instead of trusting observed action, and a repeated negative-corrective sentence formula becomes a noticeable prose tic.
